prompt: Completa: 'Penso che io ____ troppo.' (parlare)
explanation: 'I think I talk too much': Penso che io parli troppo. After an opinion verb like penso che, the verb in the che-clause goes into the present subjunctive. For -are verbs the three singular persons are all the same: io, tu and lui/lei all give parli. Build it from the stem parl- plus -i.
examiner_note: The frame 'che io' supplies the person; cue names only the verb (Rev 9 cue economy). Singular kept load-bearing via the explicit subject (identical-singulars note).

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che Maria ____ a Roma.' (abitare)
explanation: 'I think Maria lives in Rome': Credo che Maria abiti a Roma. The 3rd-person singular present subjunctive of an -are verb ends in -i: abiti. The indicative abita (with -a) is the form to avoid here; the opinion verb credo che forces the subjunctive abiti.
examiner_note: The indicative 'abita' is the signature wrong form for the -are singular subjunctive; it sits in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che i bambini non ____ durante il film.' (parlare)
explanation: 'I hope the children don't talk during the film': Spero che i bambini non parlino durante il film. The 3rd-person plural present subjunctive of an -are verb is -ino: parlino. The common slip is the indicative -ano (parlano), which differs by a single vowel; spero che pins the subjunctive.
examiner_note: The -ino vs indicative -ano contrast is the headline -are 3pl trap; parlano in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È importante che voi ____ con onestà.' (parlare)
explanation: 'It's important that you speak honestly': È importante che voi parliate con onestà. The voi present subjunctive of an -are verb is -iate: parliate, with an i the indicative voi form (parlate) does not have. È importante che is an impersonal trigger that requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Subjunctive parliate vs indicative parlate is the voi trap; parlate in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ di più.' (lavorare)
explanation: 'I want you to work more': Voglio che tu lavori di più. Voglio che (a verb of will) requires the subjunctive in the che-clause. The tu present subjunctive of an -are verb is -i: lavori. Avoid the -a form (lavora), which is the indicative 3sg and the imperative.
examiner_note: tu subjunctive lavori coincides with the indicative tu; the diagnostic miss here is the -a form, caught by lavora in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che loro ci ____ un momento.' (aspettare)
explanation: 'They need to wait a moment for us': Bisogna che loro ci aspettino un momento. Bisogna che requires the subjunctive. The -are 3rd-person plural subjunctive is -ino: aspettino, not the indicative aspettano.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che io ____ il treno delle otto.' (prendere)
explanation: 'I think I'll take the eight o'clock train': Penso che io prenda il treno delle otto. Unlike -are verbs (which take -i), -ere and -ire verbs take -a in all three singular persons of the present subjunctive: prenda. So the io form here is prenda, not the indicative prendo.
examiner_note: The -ere/-ire singular ending is -a; the -i form (prendi, the -are pattern misapplied) is the cross-class miss, in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che Luca ____ molto bene.' (scrivere)
explanation: 'I think Luca writes very well': Credo che Luca scriva molto bene. The 3rd-person singular present subjunctive of an -ere verb is -a: scriva. The indicative scrive (with -e) is the form credo che rules out.
examiner_note: Indicative scrive is the 3sg trap; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che ____ presto.' (partire, loro)
explanation: 'I hope they leave soon': Spero che partano presto. The 3rd-person plural present subjunctive of an -ere/-ire verb is -ano: partano. The frequent slip is the indicative -ono (partono); they differ by one vowel. Spero che forces the subjunctive.
examiner_note: No subject in the sentence, so the cue carries the person (partire, loro). The -ano vs indicative -ono contrast is the 3pl trap; partono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ una decisione.' (prendere)

translation.reveal: prendere - to take / to make (a decision)

explanation: 'I want you to make a decision': Voglio che tu prenda una decisione. The tu present subjunctive of an -ere verb is -a, the same as the other two singular persons: prenda. The tempting wrong form is prendi (the -i ending borrowed from -are verbs, and also the indicative tu). Voglio che requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: prendi (the -are ending on an -ere verb, also the indicative tu) is the key cross-class miss; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È bene che voi ____ almeno otto ore.' (dormire)
explanation: 'It's good that you sleep at least eight hours': È bene che voi dormiate almeno otto ore. The voi present subjunctive is -iate for every conjugation: dormiate. The indicative voi form (dormite) is the slip; the impersonal trigger è bene che requires the subjunctive.

prompt: Completa: 'Dubito che il negozio ____ la domenica.' (aprire)
explanation: 'I doubt the shop opens on Sundays': Dubito che il negozio apra la domenica. Aprire is a plain -ire verb (no -isc-), so the 3rd-person singular subjunctive is the stem plus -a: apra. Dubito che (a verb of doubt) requires the subjunctive, ruling out the indicative apre.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che io ____ bene questa regola.' (capire)
explanation: 'I don't think I understand this rule well': Non credo che io capisca bene questa regola. Capire belongs to the -isc- group: it inserts -isc- before the ending in the three singular persons. So the subjunctive singular is capisca, built the same way as the indicative capisco but with the subjunctive -a.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names the -isc- class membership, which the -ire infinitive does not reveal (criterion 15 leak case).

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che il film ____ presto.' (finire)
explanation: 'I hope the film ends soon': Spero che il film finisca presto. Finire is an -isc- verb, so the singular subjunctive inserts -isc-: finisca. The indicative finisce is the slip; spero che forces the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (names -isc- membership). Indicative finisce in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che loro ____ il mare alla montagna.' (preferire)
explanation: 'I think they prefer the seaside to the mountains': Penso che loro preferiscano il mare alla montagna. The -isc- also appears in the 3rd-person plural: preferiscano. Two things to get right at once here, the -isc- insert and the subjunctive ending -ano (not the indicative -ono of preferiscono).
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Double trap: keep -isc- and use -ano not -ono; preferiscono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che noi ____ il problema prima di decidere.' (capire)
explanation: 'We need to understand the problem before deciding': Bisogna che noi capiamo il problema prima di decidere. The -isc- insert appears in the singular and the 3rd-person plural, but never in the noi form. The noi present subjunctive is the plain stem plus -iamo: capiamo, identical to the indicative noi. Carrying the -isc- in (capisciamo) is the over-extension to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb left visible deliberately: naming the -isc- class here tempts the catchable over-extension capisciamo, which is in must_not_include (criterion 15 productive-trap case).

prompt: Completa: 'È importante che voi ____ il lavoro oggi.' (finire)
explanation: 'It's important that you finish the work today': È importante che voi finiate il lavoro oggi. Like the noi form, the voi present subjunctive of an -isc- verb has no -isc-: finiate. The two over-extensions to avoid are finisciate (keeping the insert) and finite (the plain indicative voi).
examiner_note: Breadcrumb left visible: tempts finisciate, the catchable over-extension, in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ una soluzione.' (cercare)

translation.reveal: cercare - to look for / to search

explanation: 'I want you to look for a solution': Voglio che tu cerchi una soluzione. Because the -are subjunctive ending starts with -i, a verb in -care inserts an h to keep the hard c sound: cercare gives cerchi. Without the h you would read a soft c (cerci), which is wrong.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names the rule-output 'add h', which is the skill under test (criterion 15).

prompt: Completa: 'È giusto che ____ il conto loro.' (pagare, loro)
explanation: 'It's only right that they pay the bill': È giusto che paghino il conto loro. Verbs in -gare insert an h before the -i of the subjunctive to keep the hard g: pagare gives paghino in the 3rd-person plural. Two things to land: the h (not pagino) and the -ino ending (not the indicative pagano).
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (names 'add h'). pagino misses the h; pagano is the indicative; both in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che la lezione ____ in orario.' (cominciare)
explanation: 'I hope the lesson starts on time': Spero che la lezione cominci in orario. Verbs in -ciare already carry an i that only softens the c, so when the subjunctive ending -i is added the two merge into one: cominciare gives cominci, not comincii. The indicative comincia is the other form to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (names the drop-i rule). comincii keeps the redundant i; comincia is the indicative.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che i bambini ____ più verdura.' (mangiare)
explanation: 'The children need to eat more vegetables': Bisogna che i bambini mangino più verdura. Verbs in -giare drop the soft-g i before the subjunctive ending: mangiare gives mangino in the 3rd-person plural, not mangiino. The indicative mangiano is the other slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed (drop-i rule-output). mangiino keeps the i; mangiano is indicative.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che Marco ____ alla festa stasera.' (venire)
explanation: 'I hope Marco comes to the party this evening': Spero che Marco venga alla festa stasera. For this large irregular group, build the present subjunctive from the io form of the present indicative: take vengo, drop the -o, add the ending. That gives venga for all three singular persons. Building from the infinitive (vena) misses the -g-.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names a class membership (from-1sg irregular) not derivable from the infinitive. Indicative viene in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che ____ tutti alla riunione domani.' (venire, loro)
explanation: 'I think everyone will come to the meeting tomorrow': Penso che vengano tutti alla riunione domani. The 3rd-person plural keeps the same -g- stem from vengo and takes -ano: vengano. The signature miss is the indicative vengono, which differs only in the final vowels.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl vengono is the headline from-1sg trap; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ attenzione.' (fare)
explanation: 'I want you to pay attention': Voglio che tu faccia attenzione. From the io form faccio, drop the -o and add the ending: faccia, for all three singular persons. The double c comes straight from faccio; the infinitive fare would mislead you toward fa or fai.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative fai in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È importante che i ragazzi ____ i compiti.' (fare)
explanation: 'It's important that the boys do their homework': È importante che i ragazzi facciano i compiti. The 3rd-person plural keeps the faccio stem and adds -ano: facciano. The indicative fanno is the slip to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl fanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che lui ____ dal medico.' (andare)
explanation: 'He needs to go to the doctor': Bisogna che lui vada dal medico. From the io form vado, drop the -o and add -a: vada. The infinitive andare would mislead toward a regular *andi; the io form is the safe base.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative va in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che ____ via presto.' (andare, loro)
explanation: 'I think they'll leave soon': Credo che vadano via presto. The 3rd-person plural keeps the vado stem and adds -ano: vadano. The indicative vanno is the form to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl vanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che lei ____ farlo da sola.' (potere)
explanation: 'I don't think she can do it by herself': Non credo che lei possa farlo da sola. From the io form posso, drop the -o and add -a: possa, with the double s carried over from posso. The indicative può is the slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative può in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che Anna ____ restare ancora un po'.' (volere)
explanation: 'I think Anna wants to stay a little longer': Penso che Anna voglia restare ancora un po'. From the io form voglio, drop the -o and add -a: voglia, keeping the -gli- of voglio. The indicative vuole, with its diphthong, is the form to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative vuole in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Dubito che loro ____ la verità.' (dire)
explanation: 'I doubt they're telling the truth': Dubito che loro dicano la verità. From the io form dico comes the dic- stem: the 3rd-person plural subjunctive is dicano. The indicative dicono is the slip; note the c stays hard before -ano with no need for an h.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl dicono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È meglio che lei ____ presto di casa.' (uscire)

translation.reveal: uscire - to go out / to leave

explanation: 'It's better if she leaves the house early': È meglio che lei esca presto di casa. Uscire shifts u to e in the stressed forms, visible in the io indicative esco. Build from there: esca. The infinitive would mislead toward usca; the indicative esce is the other slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative esce in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ la porta chiusa.' (tenere)
explanation: 'I want you to keep the door closed': Voglio che tu tenga la porta chiusa. Tenere has the -g- form tengo in the io present, so the subjunctive is tenga. The indicative tieni (with the diphthong) is the slip to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative tieni in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Mi dispiace che lui ____ partire così presto.' (dovere)

any_phrases.note: standard but less common variant; debba is the more usual form

explanation: 'I'm sorry he has to leave so early': Mi dispiace che lui debba partire così presto. Dovere has two standard singular subjunctive forms: debba (the more common) and deva. Both are correct; debba is preferred in careful writing. The indicative deve is the slip to avoid here, ruled out by the emotion trigger mi dispiace che.
examiner_note: debba full credit, deva graded 0.9. Flagged for the project author in coverage: confirm the credit split is house policy.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che ____ una buona idea.' (essere)
explanation: 'I think it's a good idea': Penso che sia una buona idea. Essere has its own subjunctive that cannot be derived from the indicative: sia for all three singular persons (and siamo, siate, siano). This is the single most useful form in the whole tree because sia is also the auxiliary for the compound past subjunctive. The indicative è is the slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: suppletive forms are not derivable from the infinitive or the 1sg. Indicative è and bare e in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Sembra che io ____ sempre in ritardo.' (essere)
explanation: 'It seems I'm always late': Sembra che io sia sempre in ritardo. The three singular persons of essere's subjunctive are identical: io, tu and lui/lei all give sia. Here the io form is sia, not the indicative sono. Sembra che is an impersonal trigger.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 1sg sono in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che ____ tutti contenti del risultato.' (essere, loro)
explanation: 'I hope everyone is happy with the result': Spero che siano tutti contenti del risultato. The 3rd-person plural of essere's subjunctive is siano. The indicative sono (which also serves as the io form) is the slip. The archaic sieno is no longer standard.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative sono in must_not_include; archaic sieno also rejected.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credo che lui ____ ragione.' (avere)
explanation: 'I don't think he's right': Non credo che lui abbia ragione. Avere's subjunctive is abbia in all three singular persons (and abbiamo, abbiate, abbiano), with a double b and no h. Like sia, abbia is a compound auxiliary, so it is worth knowing cold. The indicative ha is the slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative ha in must_not_include; abia (single b) is the spelling slip.

prompt: Completa: 'Voglio che tu ____ un po' di pazienza.' (avere)
explanation: 'I want you to have a little patience': Voglio che tu abbia un po' di pazienza. The tu subjunctive of avere is abbia, identical to the io and lui/lei forms. Two slips lurk here: the indicative hai, and the imperative abbi (abbi pazienza!), which is a different mood.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Note the imperative abbi is a plausible wrong form for tu; it is in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'È strano che loro non ____ ancora fame.' (avere)
explanation: 'It's strange that they're not hungry yet': È strano che loro non abbiano ancora fame. The 3rd-person plural subjunctive of avere is abbiano (double b, no h). The indicative hanno is the slip; è strano che is an emotion/evaluation trigger that requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl hanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Spero che Maria ____ la risposta giusta.' (sapere)
explanation: 'I hope Maria knows the right answer': Spero che Maria sappia la risposta giusta. Sapere's subjunctive is sappia, with a double p that the indicative (sa, sai, sa) never shows. The imperative sappi is a different mood; sapia with one p is a spelling slip.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative sa in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Bisogna che lei ti ____ una mano.' (dare)
explanation: 'She needs to give you a hand': Bisogna che lei ti dia una mano. Dare's subjunctive singular is dia (and diamo, diate, diano). The indicative dà, with its accent, is the slip; bisogna che requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative dà (and bare da) in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Mi auguro che tuo padre ____ meglio adesso.' (stare)
explanation: 'I hope your father is feeling better now': Mi auguro che tuo padre stia meglio adesso. Stare's subjunctive singular is stia (and stiamo, stiate, stiano), patterning like dare's dia. The indicative sta is the slip; mi auguro che is a wish trigger that requires the subjunctive.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative sta in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che i bambini ____ bene in quella scuola.' (stare)
explanation: 'I think the children are doing well at that school': Penso che i bambini stiano bene in quella scuola. The 3rd-person plural of stare's subjunctive is stiano. The indicative stanno is the slip to avoid.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed. Indicative 3pl stanno in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Pensavo che io ____ troppo.' (parlare)
explanation: 'I thought I talked too much': Pensavo che io parlassi troppo. A past-tense opinion verb (pensavo che) pulls the che-clause into the imperfect subjunctive. For -are verbs it is the stem plus -assi: parl- + -assi. The double s is obligatory; parlasi with one s is the standard spelling slip.
examiner_note: 1sg and 2sg are identical (parlassi), so the explicit subject 'io' carries the person; cue names only the verb (Rev 9 cue economy).

prompt: Completa: 'Speravo che Maria ____ con il direttore.' (parlare)
explanation: 'I hoped Maria would speak to the director': Speravo che Maria parlasse con il direttore. The 3rd-person singular imperfect subjunctive of an -are verb is -asse: parlasse. The imperfect indicative parlava is the form speravo che rules out.

prompt: Completa: 'Volevo che lui ____ una decisione.' (prendere)

translation.reveal: prendere - to take / to make (a decision)

explanation: 'I wanted him to make a decision': Volevo che lui prendesse una decisione. Each conjugation keeps its own class vowel before -sse: -are gives -asse, -ere gives -esse, -ire gives -isse. So prendere gives prendesse, not prendasse.
examiner_note: Wrong class vowel (prendasse) is the cross-class miss; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Era meglio che ____ un po' di più.' (dormire, loro)
explanation: 'It was better that they slept a bit more': Era meglio che dormissero un po' di più. The -ire class takes -i- before the ending, and the 3rd-person plural is -issero: dormissero. The imperfect indicative dormivano is the slip.
examiner_note: No subject in the sentence, so the cue carries the person (dormire, loro).

prompt: Completa: 'Bisognava che noi ne ____ prima.' (parlare)
explanation: 'We needed to talk about it first': Bisognava che noi ne parlassimo prima. The noi form of the imperfect subjunctive is -assimo: parlassimo, distinct from the imperfect indicative parlavamo.

prompt: Completa: 'Vorrei che voi ____ più piano.' (parlare)
explanation: 'I'd like you to speak more quietly': Vorrei che voi parlaste più piano. A present conditional main verb (vorrei che) also selects the imperfect subjunctive. Note the voi form breaks the double-s run: it is parlaste with ONE s, while every other person has two (parlassi, parlasse, parlassimo, parlassero).
examiner_note: The 2pl is the one person WITHOUT the double s; a learner over-applying the -ss- pattern writes parlassete. Homograph with the passato remoto 2pl (parlaste) is harmless here: the frame pins the subjunctive.

prompt: Completa: 'Non credevo che tu ____ così bene.' (scrivere)
explanation: 'I didn't think you wrote so well': Non credevo che tu scrivessi così bene. The 1sg and 2sg are identical (scrivessi), so the subject tu carries the person. The imperfect indicative scrivevi is the slip, and scrivesi drops the obligatory second s.

prompt: Completa: 'Se io ____ più tempo, leggerei di più.' (avere)
explanation: 'If I had more time, I'd read more': Se avessi più tempo, leggerei di più. In an unreal 'if' clause Italian uses the imperfect subjunctive, never the conditional: se avessi, not se avrei. Avere is regular here, building on the av- stem: av- + -essi.
examiner_note: se avessi is the highest-frequency imperfect subjunctive a learner meets. The 'se avrei' error (conditional in the if-clause) is the signature miss; in must_not_include. The counterfactual RULE is usage's; this item supplies the frame and tests the form only.

prompt: Completa: 'Pensavo che ____ un errore.' (essere)
explanation: 'I thought it was a mistake': Pensavo che fosse un errore. Essere is the one genuinely irregular imperfect subjunctive: fossi, fossi, fosse, fossimo, foste, fossero. It cannot be reached from the infinitive or the imperfect indicative; it is learned outright. The indicative era is the slip.

prompt: Completa: 'Speravo che ____ già arrivati.' (essere, loro)
explanation: 'I hoped they had already arrived': Speravo che fossero già arrivati. The 3rd-person plural of essere's imperfect subjunctive is fossero. The imperfect indicative erano is the slip.

prompt: Completa: 'Se ____ in te, non lo farei.' (essere, io)
explanation: 'If I were you, I wouldn't do it': Se fossi in te, non lo farei. The unreal 'if' clause takes the imperfect subjunctive, so it is se fossi, never se sarei. The 1sg and 2sg are identical (fossi), so the cue supplies the person.
examiner_note: 'Se fossi in te' is a set phrase worth knowing whole. The conditional-in-the-if-clause error (se sarei) is in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Volevo che lui ____ più attenzione.' (fare)
explanation: 'I wanted him to pay more attention': Volevo che lui facesse più attenzione. Fare restores its longer stem fac-, exactly the one the imperfect indicative uses (facevo). If you know facevo, you know facessi / facesse. Building from the short infinitive gives the wrong fasse.
examiner_note: Breadcrumb suppressed: it names the stem-expansion pattern, which the infinitive does not reveal.

prompt: Completa: 'Non pensavo che lei ____ una cosa simile.' (dire)
explanation: 'I didn't think she'd say a thing like that': Non pensavo che lei dicesse una cosa simile. Dire expands to the dic- stem, the same one behind the imperfect indicative dicevo: dic- + -esse. Watch the passato remoto disse, which is a different tense entirely.
examiner_note: disse (passato remoto) is a plausible wrong reach for a past form; in must_not_include.

prompt: Completa: 'Sarebbe stato meglio che lui ti ____ una mano.' (dare)
explanation: 'It would have been better if he'd given you a hand': Sarebbe stato meglio che lui ti desse una mano. Dare contracts to des-: desse, not the regularised dasse. The imperfect indicative dava and the passato remoto diede are the two slips.

prompt: Completa: 'Speravo che tuo padre ____ meglio.' (stare)
explanation: 'I hoped your father was doing better': Speravo che tuo padre stesse meglio. Stare patterns like dare, contracting to stes-: stesse, not the regularised stasse. Italian uses stare for how someone is feeling.

prompt: Completa: 'Era strano che lei non ____ mai vino.' (bere)
explanation: 'It was strange that she never drank wine': Era strano che lei non bevesse mai vino. Bere expands to the bev- stem, the same one as the imperfect indicative bevevo: bev- + -esse. The passato remoto bevve is a different tense.

prompt: Completa: 'Penso che Marco ____ parlato con lei.' (avere)
explanation: 'I think Marco has spoken to her': Penso che Marco abbia parlato con lei. The compound past subjunctive is the PRESENT subjunctive of the auxiliary plus the participle. So after penso che the auxiliary is abbia, not the indicative ha. The participle is unchanged.
examiner_note: Aux-only blank (the participle is supplied), so a single markpoint: the one skill is 'the auxiliary is subjunctive'. Indicative ha is the defining compound miss. Breadcrumb suppressed: the compound leaf's label embeds the worked answer (che io abbia parlato / che io avessi parlato), so it would hand the item over. Flagged to Architecture as a breadcrumb-hygiene gap (DECISIONS 2026-07-12).

prompt: Completa: 'Credo che Maria ____ già partita.' (essere)
explanation: 'I think Maria has already left': Credo che Maria sia già partita. Verbs like partire take essere, and in the past subjunctive that auxiliary goes into the present subjunctive: sia, not the indicative è.
examiner_note: Aux-only blank; participle supplied so agreement is not the test. Breadcrumb suppressed: the compound leaf's label embeds the worked answer (che io abbia parlato / che io avessi parlato), so it would hand the item over. prompt: Completa: 'Credo che Anna ____ tutta la torta.' (mangiare)
explanation: 'I think Anna ate the whole cake': Credo che Anna abbia mangiato tutta la torta. The auxiliary becomes abbia. With avere the participle does NOT agree with the subject, so it stays mangiato even though Anna is feminine. Only essere makes the participle agree with the subject.
examiner_note: The agreement trap: mangiata (agreeing with the feminine subject under avere) is the over-application, in must_not_include. Two markpoints per criterion 8: the subjunctive auxiliary and the participle are distinct skills. prompt: Completa: 'Se ____ prima, sarei venuto.' (sapere, io)
explanation: 'If I'd known earlier, I would have come': Se avessi saputo prima, sarei venuto. In an impossible past 'if' clause Italian uses the trapassato subjunctive: the IMPERFECT subjunctive of the auxiliary (avessi) plus the participle. Never the conditional in the if-clause: se avrei saputo is wrong.
examiner_note: The 'se avrei' error (conditional in the if-clause) is the signature miss; in must_not_include. The counterfactual RULE is usage's; this item supplies the frame and tests the form. Two markpoints per criterion 8: the subjunctive auxiliary and the participle are distinct skills. The participle markpoint cross-references the passato prossimo tree, which owns participle form and agreement. prompt: Completa: 'Credevo che lui ____ il film.' (vedere)

any_phrases.note: archaic; visto is the modern form

prompt: Completa: 'Non lo aiuto più. Che ____ da solo, allora!' (fare)
explanation: 'Let him manage on his own, then!': Che faccia da solo, allora! Italian has no third-person imperative, so a main clause opens with che and puts the verb in the present subjunctive to give a command about someone else. The plain indicative (Che fa) is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Se proprio vuole partire, che ____ pure!' (andare)
explanation: 'If he really wants to leave, let him go ahead!': Che vada pure! The che + subjunctive main clause carries a third-person 'let him...'. pure softens it to 'go ahead'. The indicative va is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Che ____ loro la decisione, io non c'entro.' (prendere, loro)

translation.reveal: prendere - to take / to make (a decision)

explanation: 'Let them make the decision, it's not my business': Che prendano loro la decisione. The exhortative works in the plural too: che + third-person plural subjunctive. The indicative prendono is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Il nonno è mancato ieri. Che ____ in pace.' (riposare)
explanation: 'Grandpa passed away yesterday. May he rest in peace': Che riposi in pace. The che + subjunctive can also voice a wish-command ('may he...'). riposare is a regular -are verb, so the subjunctive is riposi; the indicative riposa is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Che lo ____ chi vuole, a me non importa!' (dire)
explanation: 'Let whoever wants to say it, I don't care!': Che lo dica chi vuole! An independent che + subjunctive, with no verb of opinion in front of it. dire builds its subjunctive from dico: dica. The indicative dice is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Non la fermo io. Che ____, se vuole!' (partire)
explanation: 'I won't stop her. Let her leave, if she wants!': Che parta! partire is a plain -ire verb, so the subjunctive is parta; the che opens the exhortative main clause. The indicative parte is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Magari ____ vero! Sarebbe bellissimo.' (essere)
explanation: 'If only it were true! It would be wonderful': Magari fosse vero! Magari plus the imperfect subjunctive voices a wish about something not (yet) real. The tense does the work: fosse, not the indicative è or the conditional sarebbe.
examiner_note: Both the indicative (è) and the conditional (sarebbe) are the misses here; a wish after Magari takes the imperfect subjunctive. Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Che siccità. Magari ____ presto!' (piovere)
explanation: 'What a drought. If only it would rain soon!': Magari piovesse presto! The wish rides on the imperfect subjunctive, not the indicative piove or the conditional pioverebbe. piovere is regular: piov- + -esse.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Se solo ____ quanto mi manchi!' (sapere, tu)
explanation: 'If only you knew how much I miss you!': Se solo sapessi! Se solo, like Magari, opens an independent wish and takes the imperfect subjunctive. sapere is regular in this tense: sapessi. The indicative sai is the miss.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Magari ____ noi la lotteria!' (vincere, noi)
explanation: 'If only we won the lottery!': Magari vincessimo noi! The wish works in any person; the noi imperfect subjunctive is vincessimo. The present vinciamo and conditional vinceremmo are the misses.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Magari ____ più tempo libero!' (avere, io)
explanation: 'If only I had more free time!': Magari avessi più tempo libero! A present, out-of-reach wish takes the imperfect subjunctive: avessi, not the indicative ho or the conditional avrei.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Che io ____, il museo è chiuso il lunedì.' (sapere, io)
explanation: 'As far as I know, the museum is closed on Mondays': Che io sappia... This is a frozen hedging formula that always carries the subjunctive sappia, never the indicative so. Learn it whole.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: 'Lo finiremo, costi quel che ____!' (costare)
explanation: 'We'll finish it, cost what it may!': ...costi quel che costi! A frozen concessive: the subjunctive costi appears twice around quel che. The indicative costa breaks the formula.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: '____ come sia, dobbiamo decidere oggi.' (essere)
explanation: 'Be that as it may, we have to decide today': Sia come sia... A frozen concessive opening the sentence with the subjunctive sia. The indicative è does not belong in the formula.
examiner_note: Sentence-initial, so the answer is capitalised (Sia); the marker is case-insensitive but the prompt shows the capital. Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.

prompt: Completa: '____ come vada, non mi arrendo.' (andare)
explanation: 'However it turns out, I won't give up': Vada come vada... A frozen concessive parallel to sia come sia, built on the subjunctive vada. The indicative va breaks it.
examiner_note: Rev 27 co-credit: the one correct form evidences BOTH the usage function and its formation, so two markpoints at credit 0.5 (marks stay 1) and BOTH buckets record a full-correctness hit. The indicative in must_not is the shared miss.
